Topic: Branch A vs Branch B overall
Implementation Claim: Branch A (Docusaurus+TypeDoc) is a comprehensive docs system
Reviewer Claim: Branch A should not be merged; Branch B is the correct approach
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct. Branch A solves the wrong problem — TypeDoc doesn't extract server.tool() calls. Branch B reads actual sources of truth.
Evidence: Branch A: 0 MCP tools extracted, 0 REST endpoints documented. Branch B: 152/152 MCP tools, OpenAPI spec byte-identical copy.
Decision: Agree with reviewer: Branch A rejected, Branch B is the candidate

Implementation Claim: Branch B extracts 152 tools with params (implied: complete)
Reviewer Claim: 152/152 count is correct but regex is fragile; will silently degrade
Adjudicator Assessment: Both partially right. 152/152 count is verified. But reviewer correctly identifies fragility — coupled to code formatting, double quotes, 50-line scan window.
Evidence: Independently verified: 152 tools extracted. Regex is indeed format-dependent. No smoke test exists.
Decision: Accept Branch B extraction as working-today. Defer smoke test to follow-up. Risk is moderate, not blocking.

Reviewer Claim: Moderate risk — missing nested lists, blockquotes, images
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct and understated. Independently verified: 70 blockquotes silently dropped across guides. 37 nested list items will be flattened. Guide pages will have visible quality gaps.
Evidence: grep -cP '^>\s' across all 6 guides = 70 blockquotes. grep -cP '^\s{2,}[-*]\s' = 37 nested list items.
Decision: This is a real issue. However, it's acceptable for MVP if documented. The guide pages are supplementary — the primary value is MCP + REST API docs.

Reviewer Claim: 26 occurrences of /market-data-bridge/ hardcoded; should be a const
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct. 26 occurrences verified. Easy to extract to a const.
Decision: Required fix before merge. Trivial effort.

Reviewer Claim: Landing page card says '135' statically; should read from OpenAPI
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct but this is low severity. The number is on a landing page card, not in the API reference.
Evidence: Line 565 of script: hardcoded '135 agent actions'
Decision: Nice-to-have fix, not blocking. The MCP tool count IS dynamic.

Reviewer Claim: Missing setup-node is a reliability risk
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct in principle but overstates severity. ubuntu-latest ships Node 22. The script uses only Node built-ins.
Evidence: Node 22 is current on ubuntu-latest. Script has zero npm deps.
Decision: Nice-to-have fix. Not blocking for merge.

Reviewer Claim: Required for first-time GitHub Pages deployment
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct. This is needed for Pages to work on first run.
Evidence: No existing Pages workflows in this repo.

Reviewer Claim: No rmSync before generation; stale files persist
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is correct. If a guide page is removed, old HTML stays.
Evidence: Script uses ensureDir() but never cleans output directory.
Decision: Required fix before merge. One-line addition.

Reviewer Claim: Planning doc adds noise to docs/; move or delete
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is mostly right. The doc has ADR value but docs/ is the wrong location. However: the smaller landing set approach says defer or remove.
Decision: Remove from landing set. Not needed for the docs system to function.

Implementation Claim: 6 guides converted to HTML (implied: high value)
Reviewer Claim: Guide pages use a custom parser with known gaps (blockquotes, nested lists)
Adjudicator Assessment: Guide pages are the weakest part of the MVP. 70 blockquotes dropped, 37 nested items flattened. The MCP + REST API docs are the actual value. Guides add maintenance cost for imperfect output.
Decision: DEFER guide pages. They add bulk and parser complexity for imperfect output. The 160-line markdown parser exists solely for these. Ship MCP + REST API + architecture first.

Reviewer Claim: CDN dependency is a risk; Redoc at runtime from cdn.redoc.ly
Adjudicator Assessment: Risk is real but standard practice. Pinning to a version URL is a good follow-up.
Evidence: Redoc CDN has been stable for years. Unpinned 'latest' is the only concern.
Decision: Accept for MVP. Follow-up to pin version.

Implementation Claim: 4 well-written Mermaid diagrams in diagrams/ directory
Reviewer Claim: Well-written but duplicate architecture-diagram.html and will go stale
Adjudicator Assessment: Reviewer is right about duplication. However, .mmd source files could have standalone value separate from any docs site. Not part of the landing set decision — they're on Branch A.
Evidence: Diagrams describe real architecture accurately but are hand-maintained and parallel to existing assets.
Decision: Do not merge as part of docs automation. Could be a separate, independent PR if wanted.
